description_rules: Provide context: what this is, why it exists, how it fits with other components
Include enough detail for an agent to implement without guessing
Do NOT put verifiable outcomes here — those belong in acceptance_criteria
acceptance_criteria_rules: Each criterion must be something you can verify by looking at a screen, inspecting a database, or performing an action
Never restate the task name as a criterion
Be specific: name columns, endpoints, error messages, status codes, UI elements
One-sentence test: if you can answer 'did this happen? yes/no' it is a good criterion
anti_patterns: BAD: 'Create the payments table' → GOOD: 'payments table exists with columns: id, amount, currency, status, created_at'
BAD: 'Handle errors gracefully' → GOOD: 'Invalid email returns 400 with message: Invalid email format'
BAD: 'Set up authentication' → GOOD: 'POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns 200 and a JWT token'
BAD: 'Implement the dashboard' → GOOD: 'Dashboard page renders a table of recent orders with columns: date, customer, total, status'

tasks.name: Create users table with auth fields (DATABASE)
tasks.description: Create the users table that stores account credentials and profile data. This is the foundational table — all other entities reference it. The table must support email/password authentication and track when accounts were created and last active.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: users table exists with columns: id (uuid, PK), email (text, unique, not null), password_hash (text, not null), display_name (text), created_at (timestamptz), last_sign_in_at (timestamptz)
Migration file can be applied to a clean database without errors
A row with a duplicate email cannot be inserted

tasks.name: Configure ESLint and Prettier for the project (INFRASTRUCTURE)
tasks.description: Set up code quality tooling so that all developers and agents produce consistent code. ESLint enforces code correctness rules. Prettier enforces formatting. Both must work with the project's TypeScript and React configuration.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: .eslintrc config file exists at project root with TypeScript and React plugins enabled
.prettierrc config file exists at project root with singleQuote: true and trailingComma: all
Running the lint command exits with code 0 on all existing source files
Running the format command exits with code 0 on all existing source files

tasks.name: Implement user login page with email and password (FEATURE)
tasks.description: Create the login page that allows existing users to authenticate. The page should have a form with email and password fields, submit to the auth API, and redirect to the dashboard on success. Error messages should be shown inline below the form.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Login page renders at /login with email input, password input, and a Submit button
Submitting invalid credentials shows the message 'Invalid email or password' below the form without a page reload
Submitting with an empty email or password field shows 'This field is required' below the empty field
The Submit button is disabled and shows a spinner while the auth request is in flight

tasks.name: Fix navigation crash on mobile viewports (BUG)
tasks.description: The navigation component throws a React hydration error when the browser viewport is below 768px. This happens because the server renders the desktop layout but the client renders the mobile layout, causing a mismatch. The fix should ensure both server and client render the same initial markup.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Navigation component renders without console errors at viewport widths 320px, 768px, and 1024px
No 'Text content did not match' or 'Hydration failed' warnings appear in the browser console at any viewport size
Mobile menu toggle button is visible and functional at viewports below 768px

tasks.name: Create organisation settings page (FEATURE)
tasks.description: Build the settings page where organisation admins can update their organisation's name, description, and contact email. The page reads from and writes to the organisations table. Changes are saved via a PATCH endpoint.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Settings page renders at /org/[orgId]/settings with pre-filled form fields for name, description, and contact_email
Clicking Save sends a PATCH request to /api/orgs/[orgId] with the updated fields
A success toast appears after a successful save
If the PATCH returns a 4xx or 5xx error, an error toast appears with the server's error message

tasks.notes: Blocked: depends on organisations table (task 2.2.1) and PATCH /api/orgs endpoint (task 2.2.2)
